name: Exchanging my talents for traveling in Ireland.
curatorsInsight:
DRAWING IRELAND PROJECT
What began as a simple question — "Can I exchange my artistic talents for accommodations while traveling Ireland?" — grew into one of the most meaningful journeys of my life.
Inspired by HelpX, a platform connecting budget travelers with hosts in need of practical help, I reimagined the concept through an artistic lens. Rather than offering manual labor, I proposed something more personal: my drawings. To bring the idea to life, I built a dedicated website where hosts could learn about the project and apply to participate.
The response led me to three remarkable hosts across Ireland. I spent time at Gallic Kitchen, a beloved Irish restaurant in Abbeyleix; Fheoirfurniture, an intimate furniture design studio; and Camphill Grangemockler, a volunteer community dedicated to people with special needs. In each place, I created artwork in exchange for a roof, a meal, and the warmth of genuine human connection.
What I brought home was more than a portfolio — it was a collection of nostalgic, irreplaceable memories shaped by the people who opened their doors to me.
historicalNarrative: This project documents Eungshin Kim's real-life experience of using artistic skills as a form of currency while traveling in Ireland. It represents the intersection of art and life, demonstrating how creative talents can open doors to cultural exchange and adventure.
